A TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF ROUTE 316 IN NEW FRANKLIN IN A HEAVILY WOODED AREA. MORE THAN A DOZEN PINE TREES WERE SNAPPED OFF. THE TORNADO DAMAGE AREA WAS LOCATED ON THE SOUTHERN EDGE OF A MUCH LARGER AREA OF DOWNBURST WIND DAMAGE WHICH EXTENDED FROM JUST WEST OF ROUTE 81 IN THE MARION AREA EASTWARD THROUGH BROWNSVILLE. BASED ON THE SEVERITY OF TREE DAMAGE IN THE AREA...MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH...WHICH RATES THIS AN EF1 TORNADO ON THE ENHANCED FUJITA SCALE.
TRACTOR TRAILER BLOWN OVER ON I 81 NEAR WAYNE EXIT. ROAD SIGNS ALSO BLOWN OVER

MULTIPLE TREES DOWN

THE TORNADO BEGAN ABOUT 1.5 MILES SOUTH SOUTHWEST OF SLATE RUN NEAR STATE ROUTE 414 AND MOVED NORTHEAST THROUGH SLATE RUN TO ABOUT A HALF MILE SOUTH OF CEDAR RUN NEAR PINE CREEK. ABOUT 400 TREES WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED AND SEVERAL SEASONAL HOMES WERE AFFECTED BY THE DOWNED TREES. ADDITIONALLY THERE WERE MULTIPLE TREES DOWN THROUGHOUT THE AREA AS A RESULT OF STRAIGHT LINE WINDS FROM THE THUNDERSTORM.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN
NUMEROUS 6 INCH THICK BRANCHES DOWN AND A FEW LARGE TREES SNAPPED. MEASURED WIND GUST OF 55 MPH.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN

THE TORNADO BEGAN JUST NORTHWEST OF CAMMAL NEAR PINE CREEK AND STATE ROUTE 414 AND MOVED NORTHEAST NEAR MILL RUN ROAD TO ABOUT 2 MILES NORTH OF CAMMAL. ABOUT 200 TREES WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. SEVERAL SEASONAL HOMES WERE AFFECTED BY DOWNED TREES. ADDITIONALLY THERE WERE MULTIPLE TREES DOWN THROUGHOUT THE AREA AS A RESULT OF STRAIGHT LINE WINDS FROM THE THUNDERSTORM.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AROUND 715 PM EDT AROUND ONE QUARTER MILE EAST OF DAUPHIN BOROUGH ON RIVER ROAD. THE STORM LIFTED ABOUT 1.5 MILES EAST OF DAUPHIN BOROUGH ON SECOND MOUNTAIN. SIX HOMES SUFFERED MODERATE TO MAJOR DAMAGE...AND 150 TREES WERE DOWNED. STORM DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENT WITH MAXIMUM WIND GUSTS AROUND 90 MPH...WHICH RATES THE TORNADO AN EF1 ON THE ENHANCED FUJITA SCALE. IN ADDITION TO THE TORNADIC WIND DAMAGE...SOME DOWNBURST STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE OCCURRED ALONG THE RIVER.
8 TREES IN YARD SNAPPED AND THROWN

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR ROUTE 114 SOUTH OF WILLOW MILL PARK AROUND 720 PM EDT AND TRAVELED NORTHEAST FOR ABOUT 2.5 MILES...OCCASIONALLY LIFTING OFF THE GROUND DURING THE PATH. THE TORNADO CROSSED ROUTE 81...THEN ENDED NEAR SEARS RUN BEFORE REACHING ROUTE 944 AROUND 724 PM EDT. THE TORNADO RESULTED IN ABOUT 100 TREES BEING UPROOTED OR SNAPPED ALONG ITS PATH. SOME MINOR DAMAGE TO HOMES WAS OBSERVED. A SMALL OUTBUILDING WAS SEVERELY DAMAGED ALONG LAMBS GAP ROAD NEAR THE END OF THE TORNADO PATH.

A TORNADO WAS DETERMINED TO HAVE INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN AT 815 PM EDT AROUND ONE HALF MILE WEST OF CRESSONA ALONG BEAVER CREEK ROAD. THE TORNADO PRODUCED A SPORADIC PATH OF DAMAGE SPANNING 18 MILES...LIFTING AND TOUCHING DOWN SEVERAL ADDITIONAL TIMES BEFORE FINALLY DISSIPATING AROUND 835 PM EDT ABOUT ONE MILE EAST OF LEIBEYVILLE IN WEST PENN TOWNSHIP. AROUND 20 HOMES WERE DAMAGED...4 OF WHICH SUFFERED MAJOR DAMAGE. IN ADDITION...ONE DOZEN BARNS AND OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED. THE TORNADO DOWNED

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORNADO IN KNOXVILLE IN TIOGA COUNTY FROM A STORM THAT CROSSED THE AREA EARLY MONDAY MORNING. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON FORK HILL...ABOUT HALF A MILE NORTHWEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF ROUTE 249 AND ROUTE 49. THE TORNADO TRAVELED EASTWARD ACROSS KNOXVILLE...JUST TO THE NORTH OF ROUTE 49...BEFORE LIFTING ROUGHLY HALF A MILE EAST OF ACADEMY CORNERS.
